title: Not specifying a timeout for regular expressions is security-sensitive
description: Not specifying a timeout for regular expressions is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotcweregexNot specifying a timeout for regular expressions can lead to a Denial-of-Service attack. Pass a timeout when usingSystem.Text.RegularExpressionsto process untrusted input because a malicious user might craft a value for which the evaluation lasts excessively long.Ask Yourself Whetherthe input passed to the regular expression is untrusted.the regular expression contains patterns vulnerable tocatastrophic backtracking.There is a risk if you answered yes to any of those questions.Recommended Secure Coding PracticesIt is recommended to specify amatchTimeoutwhen executing a regular expression.Make sure regular expressions are not vulnerable to Denial-o
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: CallExpression nodes that match Regex method calls AND do not contain TimeSpan timeout parameter
logic.reasoning: Detects Regex operations without timeout specifications. Uses AST node targeting of CallExpression to identify Regex method calls, then applies regex pattern to detect Regex constructors/method calls while excluding timeout-aware calls and safe contexts like logging/testing. Exclude patterns ensure it doesn't trigger on safe contexts.
